cases.title: Clef des Champs
cases.intro: A farm-to-table identity rooted in soil, season, and ceremony — built to feel as alive as the produce it represents.

sections.heading: An identity that tastes like the land
sections.body: We were asked to build a brand that holds both heritage and modernity — handmade in feel, yet able to scale across packaging, signage, and digital. The work began in the fields and ended in a flexible visual language.

items.heading: Approach
items.body: Strategy first. We mapped the rituals of harvest into a modular grid that flexes from a jar label to a billboard.

items.heading: Outcome
items.body: A 30% lift in shelf recognition and a system the in-house team can extend without us.

cases.title: Ditchfield
cases.intro: A heritage roastery rebrand — slow craft, deep warmth, and a wordmark built to age like the beans it sells.

sections.heading: Old soul, new shelf presence
sections.body: Ditchfield needed to feel rooted without feeling dated. We leaned into a confident serif wordmark and a palette of roasted browns and cream.

cases.title: Cinéma Cinéma
cases.intro: A repertory cinema brand that turns every screening into an event — bold, cinematic, and unapologetically loud.

sections.heading: Double feature energy
sections.body: We doubled everything — the name, the marks, the marquee — into a system that feels like a title card for an indie classic.

items.heading: Identity
items.body: A flexible marquee system that adapts to any film, any night.

items.heading: Motion
items.body: Title-card animations that bookend every screening.

cases.title: Sovimage
cases.intro: A production house web platform built to showcase the reel first — fast, dark, and cinematic.

sections.heading: Let the work play
sections.body: Every decision served the footage. A near-black canvas, oversized type, and a player-first layout put the reel centre stage.

cases.title: Quartier Molson
cases.intro: A neighbourhood brand for a former brewery district — industrial heritage reframed as a place to live and gather.

sections.heading: From brewery to belonging
sections.body: We built a wayfinding-led identity that honours the brick-and-copper past while signalling a warm, modern community.

cases.title: Maison Aurore
cases.intro: A fashion house identity poised between couture restraint and a dawn-lit optimism.

sections.heading: Light, made wearable
sections.body: Aurore means dawn. The system trades on soft gradients, generous space, and a wordmark that feels stitched rather than set.

cases.title: Lumara Hotels
cases.intro: A luxury hospitality identity designed to feel quiet, warm, and unmistakably modern.

sections.heading: Understated luxury, end to end
sections.body: From wayfinding to the booking flow, every touchpoint was tuned for calm. The wordmark sets the tone — a slow, confident italic that reads as effortless.

cases.title: Soluna Labs
cases.intro: A deep-tech startup brand engineered for trust — precise, technical, and quietly futuristic.

sections.heading: Make the complex feel inevitable
sections.body: Soluna needed to look like it had already won. We built a monochrome system with a single electric-green accent and a grid that reads as engineering.

items.heading: Brand
items.body: A confident monospace-adjacent wordmark and a strict mono palette.

items.heading: Web
items.body: A fast, dark product site that turns specs into story.
